libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property is indicated. Tests should account for the 2.0.0 API changes: use DocumentRenderingContext instead of the removed RenderingContext, avoid removed logging/default-template setters, and obtain SiteRenderer through component lookup or otherwise initialize its injected collaborators before exercising render/locate/merge paths. Create temporary site and skin resources in test code.

deterministic_setup.reason: doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 marks maven-artifact as provided, but core public skin APIs use Artifact/DefaultArtifact when creating SiteRenderingContext for a skin.
Upstream explicitly notes parser modules are no longer in doxia-core and must be stated so component descriptors are picked up; APT is a normal Doxia site source format.
XDOC rendering is a normal site-renderer path and is test-scoped upstream, so the generated scaffold will not receive its parser component transitively.
FML/FAQ rendering is a normal site-renderer path and is test-scoped upstream, so the generated scaffold will not receive its parser component transitively.

DefaultSiteRenderer has injected Doxia, ParserModuleManager, VelocityComponent, and PlexusContainer fields; upstream tests obtain SiteRenderer from a Plexus test container instead of direct construction.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 1.9.1 tests fail javac primarily because public APIs changed in 2.0.0; setup alone will not fix those source incompatibilities.
Parser-module component discovery and Velocity/Sisu wiring may affect native-image behavior, so local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful coverage of doxia-site-renderer 2.0.0 usually requires its Maven/Sisu component wiring plus parser modules that are test-scoped in the upstream POM, not pulled in by the normal scaffold.
